title: Ceramics 3D

what.content:
You can see inside the rim, underneath the foot, around the curve — ceramic forms that only make sense in 3D, now spinnable in a browser with a click.

[View 3D Album on Polycam](https://poly.cam/album/81A58AD0-B215-41DE-8277-FA3A21A26AAD)
Once scanned, ceramic pieces can be embedded directly into websites like these below, and people can click to rotate and see them from all angles. Click/scroll around this one, below:

why.content: Ceramicists work in three dimensions - but they display their work online in two. Whether selling their pieces or applying to residencies, the world of ceramics documentation is primarily flat. But these forms cannot often be expressed in 2D: the forms make sense only when you can see inside, around, and underneath them.

how.content:
Place ceramic on turntable in room with consistent, soft lighting

Take 100-200 photos while raising and lowering the phone, slowly spinning turntable

Process on Polycam, an iPhone app

scraped_raw_text: Ceramicists work in three dimensions - but they communicate their work in two. Whether selling their pieces online or applying to residencies, the world of ceramics documentation is primarily flat. But these forms cannot often be expressed in 2D: the forms make sense only when you can see inside, around, and underneath them.
This project involved photogrammetry scanning 15 pieces by ceramicist Julia Mattis for use online.
Once scanned, ceramic pieces can be embedded directly into websites like the four below, and viewers can click to rotate and see them from all angles.
Place ceramic on turntable in room with consistent, soft lighting
Take 100-200 photos while raising and lowering the phone, slowly spinning turntable
Process on Polycam, an iPhone app
